questions.text: Which of the following is an example of rectilinear motion?
options: Motion of a child on a swing
Motion of a car on a straight road
questions.explanation: Rectilinear motion is motion along a straight line.

questions.text: Which of the following is a periodic motion?
questions.explanation: Periodic motion repeats itself at regular intervals of time.

questions.text: Speed of an object is defined as:
questions.explanation: Speed is the rate at which distance is covered.

questions.text: The basic unit of speed is:
questions.explanation: Meters per second (m/s) is the SI unit for speed.

questions.text: The basic unit of time is:
questions.explanation: Second (s) is the SI base unit for time.

questions.text: An instrument used to measure the distance travelled by a vehicle is:
questions.explanation: Odometer records the total distance covered by the vehicle.

questions.text: The instrument used to measure the speed of a vehicle is:
questions.explanation: Speedometer shows the instantaneous speed of the vehicle.

questions.text: One full to-and-fro motion of a pendulum is called an:
questions.explanation: The pendulum swings from one extreme position to another and back.

questions.text: The time taken by a pendulum to complete one oscillation is called its:
questions.explanation: Time period remains constant for a fixed length of the pendulum.

questions.text: A car covers 100 km in 2 hours. Its speed is:
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance / Time = 100 / 2 = 50 km/h.

questions.text: The distance-time graph for an object moving with a constant speed is a:
questions.explanation: A straight line starting from origin indicates uniform speed.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT correct?
questions.explanation: Distance is the product of speed and time.

questions.text: Earlier, time was measured using ______.
questions.explanation: People used periodic events in nature like sunrise or moon phases before mechanical clocks.

questions.text: Galileo Galilei found that the time period of a pendulum depends on its:
questions.explanation: Increasing the length of the string increases the time period.

questions.text: 36 km/h is equal to ______ m/s.
Non-uniform motion is when an object:
questions.explanation: In non-uniform motion, the object covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.text: A microsecond is one ______ of a second.
questions.explanation: 1 microsecond = 10^-6 seconds.

questions.text: Quartz clocks are more ______ than pendulum clocks.
questions.explanation: Electronic circuits in quartz clocks provide very precise timekeeping.

questions.text: If an object is at rest, its distance-time graph is a:
options: Horizontal line parallel to time axis
questions.explanation: Distance does not change as time increases.

questions.text: Distance is measured in ______ in the SI system.
questions.explanation: Meter (m) is the standard unit of length.

questions.text: A nanosecond is one-billionth of a:
questions.explanation: Used for measuring extremely short time intervals in science.

questions.text: An object covers 15 meters in 1 second. Its speed is:
questions.explanation: Speed = 15/1 = 15 m/s.

questions.text: What does a horizontal line on a distance-time graph indicate?
questions.explanation: Zero slope means zero speed.

questions.text: Average speed is used because most objects:
questions.explanation: Real-world motion often involves starting, stopping, and varying speeds.

questions.text: Distance = ______ × Time
questions.explanation: Fundamental formula of kinematics.

questions.text: Which of these is a periodic event useful for measuring time?
questions.explanation: Sunrise is predictable and occurs at regular intervals.

questions.text: The slope of a distance-time graph represents:
questions.explanation: A steeper slope means a higher speed.

questions.text: If a car moves at 72 km/h, how much distance will it cover in 10 seconds?
questions.explanation: 72 km/h = 20 m/s. Distance = 20 * 10 = 200 m.

questions.text: Age of a person is expressed in:
questions.explanation: Years is a convenient larger unit of time.

questions.text: A year is the time taken by Earth to complete one ______ around the sun.
questions.explanation: This takes approximately 365.25 days.

questions.text: A day is the time taken by Earth to complete one ______ on its axis.
questions.explanation: One rotation takes 24 hours.

questions.text: The time between two consecutive new moons is a:
questions.explanation: A lunar month is about 29.5 days.

questions.text: Simple pendulum is a mass (bob) suspended by a string that ______.
questions.explanation: It shows to-and-fro motion.

questions.text: A faster vehicle covers a ______ distance in a given time.
questions.explanation: Higher speed means more distance per unit time.

questions.text: Units of speed like m/min or km/min are ______.
questions.explanation: They can be used depending on the context of the motion.

questions.text: In a distance-time graph, time is usually plotted on the ______ axis.
questions.explanation: Time is the independent variable.

questions.text: In a distance-time graph, distance is plotted on the ______ axis.
questions.explanation: Distance is the dependent variable.

questions.text: A pendulum bob is at rest in its ______ position.
questions.explanation: The mean position is the equilibrium position in the middle.

questions.text: Sundials measure time by the position of the ______.
questions.explanation: Shadow cast by the sun changes position throughout the day.

questions.text: Is constant speed same as uniform motion?
questions.explanation: Uniform motion means speed is constant throughout the journey.
